focus.description: Identify the shapes of two-dimensional cross-sections of three-dimensional objects, and identify three-dimensional objects generated by rotations of two-dimensional objects.

ancestors.description: Describe the two-dimensional figures that result from slicing three-dimensional figures, as in plane sections of right rectangular prisms and right rectangular pyramids.

descendants.description: (+) Give an informal argument using Cavalieri's principle for the formulas for the volume of a sphere and other solid figures.

learningComponents.description: Identify the object generated by rotating a two-dimensional figure in space
learningComponents.author: Achievement Network

learningComponents.description: Identify the shape of a cross-section of a three-dimensional object, including prisms, pyramids, cylinders, cones, and spheres
learningComponents.author: Achievement Network
